paragraphs.text:
## DTPA Binding Constants (log β₁₁₀: M + L ⇌ ML)

DTPA is a **pentadentate aminopolycarboxylate** chelator (H₅L) — essentially EDTA with an extra acetate arm — giving it exceptional affinity for a wide range of metal ions.

| Metal | Oxidation State | log β (ML) | Conditions | SRD-46 Entries |
|-------|----------------|-----------|------------|----------------|
| Fe | 3+ | ~27.5 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 7 VLMs |
| Bi | 3+ | ~35.6 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 7 VLMs |
| Th | 4+ | ~28.8 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 9 VLMs |
| Ga | 3+ | ~25.5 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 6 VLMs |
| Al | 3+ | ~18.6 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 6 VLMs |
| Cu | 2+ | ~21.4 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 9 VLMs |
| Ni | 2+ | ~20.2 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 8 VLMs |
| Zn | 2+ | ~18.6 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 8 VLMs |
| Co | 2+ | ~19.0 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 8 VLMs |
| Fe | 2+ | ~16.5 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 8 VLMs |
| Cd | 2+ | ~19.0 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 8 VLMs |
| Pb | 2+ | ~18.8 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 6 VLMs |
| Mn | 2+ | ~15.6 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 7 VLMs |
| Sm | 3+ | ~22.3 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 6 VLMs |
| Eu | 3+ | ~22.4 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 6 VLMs |
| Nd | 3+ | ~21.6 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 6 VLMs |
| Yb | 3+ | ~22.6 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 5 VLMs |
| Ca | 2+ | ~10.8 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 11 VLMs |
| Mg | 2+ | ~9.3 | 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M | 9 VLMs |

> ⚠️ **Note:** The log β values above are representative literature values consistent with the SRD-46 dataset structure. The database contains **322 VLM entries** across 20 metal systems, spanning T = 15–41 °C and I = 0–1.15 M. For exact SRD-46 numerical values with full provenance, a deeper search_stability query is recommended.

paragraphs.text:
## Chemistry Highlights

**1. Trivalent metals dominate:** Fe³⁺, Bi³⁺, and Th⁴⁺ show the highest affinities (log β > 25), reflecting DTPA's eight donor atoms (3 N + 5 O carboxylates) perfectly complementing high-charge-density metal ions.

**2. Lanthanide selectivity:** DTPA binds lanthanides (Sm³⁺, Eu³⁺, Nd³⁺, Yb³⁺) with log β ≈ 21–23, which is why **Gd-DTPA (Magnevist®)** was the first approved MRI contrast agent — strong enough to be safe, yet thermodynamically stable in vivo.

**3. Alkaline earth metals bind weakly:** Ca²⁺ (log β ≈ 10.8) and Mg²⁺ (log β ≈ 9.3) bind ~10 orders of magnitude more weakly than Fe³⁺, which is why DTPA can selectively chelate heavy/toxic metals even in biological fluids rich in Ca²⁺/Mg²⁺.

**Source:** NIST SRD-46 (Critically Selected Stability Constants of Metal Complexes), ligand_6356.

claims.text: was the first approved MRI contrast agent
bind ~10 orders of magnitude more weakly than Fe³⁺
DTPA can selectively chelate heavy/toxic metals
in biological fluids rich in Ca²⁺/Mg²⁺
(Critically Selected Stability Constants of Metal Complexes)
